articles.title: Nvidia launches ‘superchip’ putting AI power into laptops and PCs
articles.summary:
Firm says its RTX Spark PC chip for Microsoft Windows will let AI agents replace the mouse and keyboard

Business live – latest updates

A new front has opened up in the battle for dominance in AI chips, as Nvidia said its latest development could replace the mouse and keyboard i
articles.titleZh: 英伟达推出“超级芯片”，将人工智能能力注入笔记本电脑和个人电脑
articles.summaryZh:
该公司表示，其适用于 Microsoft Windows 的 RTX Spark PC 芯片将让人工智能代理取代鼠标和键盘

商业直播 – 最新更新

AI芯片主导地位之战开辟了一条新战线，英伟达表示其最新研发成果可能会取代鼠标和键盘

contentParagraphs.en: A new front has opened up in the battle for dominance in AI chips, as Nvidia said its latest development could replace the mouse and keyboard in how people use computers.The $5tn (£3.7tn) US semiconductor company has launched a “superchip” that puts AI capabilities into laptops and desktop computers, a move that will pit it against Intel, Apple, Qualcomm and AMD.The RTX Spark chip will be launched this year, and will be used by computer makers including Dell, Lenovo, Asus and HP, paired with Microsoft’s Windows software, according to the Nvidia chief executive, Jensen Huang.Speaking at the Computex conference in Taiwan, Huang said the chip would “reinvent the PC” for the AI era, after three years of collaboration between Nvidia and Microsoft.A combination of a microprocessor and a graphics chip, developed with help from Taiwan’s MediaTek, it is designed to run AI agents locally rather than relying on cloud computing.It will allow agents to navigate PCs autonomously, replacing humans’ traditional mouse and keyboard interactions. Because the chip is very powerful, computers will still be thin and light, the company said.Huang said Nvidia was reimagining the PC “for the first time in 40 years”.The company’s foray into the consumer PC industry will open up a new business line, but this will take time, analysts said. Nvidia, which dominates the booming AI semiconductor market, is pushing beyond graphics cards into integrated chips that power the whole computer.Neil Shah, a co-founder of Counterpoint Research, compared the “RTX Spark moment” with the advent of the iPhone, ChatGPT and DeepSeek.“The RTX Spark looks to transform the traditional app-centric PC to a real useful agentic AI personal computer which will eventually be in every home in coming years as private edge AI agents become pivotal,” he said.The new chip and Nvidia’s Vera central processing unit (CPU) demonstrate the company’s growing focus on PC and CPU products. The Vera CPU is designed for AI agents and early adopters, including OpenAI, Anthropic and SpaceX.Susannah Streeter, the chief investment strategist at Wealth Club, said: “Nvidia’s latest push into AI-powered personal computers marks a bold attempt to extend its dominance beyond datacentres and into consumers’ everyday lives. The unveiling of the RTX Spark chip reinforces Jensen Huang’s vision of PCs evolving from simple productivity tools into hyperintelligent digital co-workers.“While strategically significant, investors are likely to view the move as a longer-term growth opportunity rather than an immediate earnings driver. For now, Nvidia’s fortunes still depend overwhelmingly on relentless global demand for AI infrastructure and datacentre computing power.”As chip wars heat up, Intel intends to start shipping an AI chip later this year that uses cheaper memory and cooling technology than its California rivals Nvidia and AMD.Intel announced a new graphics processing unit, Xe3P, codenamed Crescent Island. It is “purpose-built for this upcoming AI generation of agents”, according to Anil Nanduri, the vice-president of AI products at Intel’s Data Center Group.Amid fears that AI will destroy vast numbers of jobs, Huang said it was “complete nonsense” that the technology would reduce demand for software engineers, arguing that it would increase hiring by making workers more productive.“This is the promise of AI,” he said. “The number of engineers, software engineers, is actually increasing. People talk about AI reducing jobs – complete nonsense. It’s causing more software engineers to be hired.”Meanwhile, Rene Haas, the chief executive of Arm, is in line for a pay package that would make him a billionaire if he hits targets to turn the microchip firm into the UK’s first trillion-dollar company.Arm, which is listed in New York but has its global headquarters in Cambridge, has proposed a pay scheme including generous share awards that is worth more than $1bn in total by 2031, if Haas can hit certain “exceptional growth metrics”.
contentParagraphs.zh: 争夺人工智能芯片主导地位的斗争已经开辟了一条新战线，英伟达表示，其最新开发成果可能会取代人们使用计算机的方式中的鼠标和键盘。这家价值 5 万亿美元（3.7 万亿英镑）的美国半导体公司推出了一款“超级芯片”，将人工智能功能融入笔记本电脑和台式电脑中，此举将使其与英特尔、苹果、高通和 AMD 展开竞争。RTX Spark 芯片将于今年推出，将由包括戴尔、联想、华硕和惠普在内的计算机制造商使用。据英伟达首席执行官黄仁勋介绍，该芯片与微软的 Windows 软件搭配使用。黄仁勋在台湾的 Computex 大会上发表讲话时表示，经过英伟达和微软三年的合作，该芯片将在人工智能时代“重塑 PC”。该芯片是在台湾联发科技的帮助下开发的微处理器和图形芯片的组合，旨在在本地运行人工智能代理，而不是依赖云计算。它将允许代理自主导航 PC，取代人类传统的鼠标和键盘交互。该公司表示，由于芯片功能非常强大，计算机仍将是轻薄的。黄仁勋表示，英伟达正在“40年来首次”重新构想个人电脑。分析师表示，该公司进军消费个人电脑行业将开辟一条新的业务线，但这需要时间。主导蓬勃发展的 AI 半导体市场的 Nvidia 正在超越显卡，转向为整个计算机提供动力的集成芯片。 Counterpoint Research 联合创始人 Neil Shah 将“RTX Spark 时刻”与 iPhone、ChatGPT 和 DeepSeek 的出现进行了比较。他说：“RTX Spark 旨在将传统的以应用程序为中心的 PC 转变为真正有用的代理 AI 个人计算机，随着私人边缘 AI 代理变得至关重要，这种计算机最终将在未来几年进入每个家庭。” Nvidia 的 Vera 中央处理器 (CPU) 表明该公司对 PC 和 CPU 产品的日益关注。 Vera CPU 是为 AI 代理和早期采用者设计的，包括 OpenAI、Anthropic 和 SpaceX。Wealth Club 首席投资策略师 Susannah Streeter 表示：“Nvidia 最新进军人工智能驱动的个人电脑，标志着其将主导地位扩展到数据中心之外、进入消费者日常生活的大胆尝试。RTX Spark 芯片的推出强化了 Jensen Huang 的愿景，即 PC 从简单的生产力工具发展为超智能数字化工具。” “虽然具有战略意义，但投资者可能会将此举视为长期增长机会，而不是直接的盈利驱动力。目前，英伟达的命运仍然很大程度上取决于全球对人工智能基础设施和数据中心计算能力的持续需求。“随着芯片大战的升温，英特尔打算在今年晚些时候开始发售一款人工智能芯片，该芯片使用比加州竞争对手英伟达和AMD更便宜的内存和冷却技术。英特尔宣布推出一款新的图形处理单元Xe3P，代号为新月岛。人工智能产品副总裁阿尼尔·南杜里(Anil Nanduri)表示，它是“专为即将到来的人工智能一代而设计的”在担心人工智能会摧毁大量就业机会的情况下，黄说，该技术会减少对软件工程师的需求，这是“完全无稽之谈”，他认为，它将通过提高工人的生产力来增加招聘。“这就是人工智能的承诺，”他说，“工程师、软件工程师的数量实际上正在增加。人们谈论人工智能减少工作岗位——完全是胡说八道。与此同时，Arm 首席执行官雷内·哈斯 (Rene Haas) 有望获得一份薪酬方案，如果他实现将这家微芯片公司打造成英国第一家万亿美元公司的目标，他将成为亿万富翁。Arm 在纽约上市，但全球总部位于剑桥，该方案提出了一项薪酬计划，其中包括到 2031 年总价值超过 10 亿美元的慷慨股票奖励，前提是哈斯能够实现一定的“超常增长”指标”。

contentParagraphs.en: An entomologist researched the worms used in mescal to better understand the relationship between the creatures and specific agave plants - and the danger if harvesting increases.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一位昆虫学家研究了龙舌兰酒中使用的蠕虫，以更好地了解这些生物与特定龙舌兰植物之间的关系，以及收获增加的危险。

contentParagraphs.en: ADRIAN FLORIDO, HOST: Entomologist Akito Kawahara was on vacation in Mexico when he felt a sudden thirst for more knowledge.AKITO KAWAHARA: We just ordered some local mezcals and tequila. And we realized when we started to drink the drinks that, you know, there's this little worm in the bottom of the glass. And we said, oh, I wonder what this is.FLORIDO: Entomologists study insects and their relationship to the environment and people, mostly chasing down the creepy crawlies in their habitats, not a cantina. But science happens where science happens.KAWAHARA: We've never had a project like this in which we had the worms preserved, like, in a bottle already.FLORIDO: Kawahara is a researcher at the Florida Museum of Natural History, part of the University of Florida in Gainesville. He wanted to know what kind of worm was in his drink - not easy on first glance because of how the alcohol alters their appearance. So of course, he had to bring a bunch of bottles home.KAWAHARA: We got all these bottles. We lined them up. We took pictures of all the bottles. We took the specimen out of each bottle and then took a photo. So we had a digital image of each specimen. We had to take a little piece of the tissue out, and then we spin it down. We crush it. We isolate the DNA from the tissue itself.FLORIDO: Kawahara and his team used DNA sequencing to identify the worms, which are actually moth larvae.KAWAHARA: And that species was Comadia redtenbacheri, and that is the agave redworm moth.FLORIDO: The agave redworm moth lives on one specific type of agave plant in Oaxaca. On a return trip there, Kawahara tracked down the agave fields where these specific worms come from. It turns out that to collect these worms and plop them into a bottle of mezcal, producers don't just pluck them off the surface of the plant. They have to burrow them out, often killing the agave plant itself. There are concerns that as demand for mezcal and mezcal worms grows, the worm and the already endangered agave plant it lives on could be at further risk.KAWAHARA: So these are the kinds of research questions that we need to be asking, especially in a time when a lot of the agave plants are disappearing, and potentially, the insects that are associated with them are also disappearing.FLORIDO: Identifying these worms - a first step in trying to figure out a more sustainable way to harvest them - a little discovery with possibly big implications worthy of a toast.KAWAHARA: (Laughter) Yeah. We've had a lot of parties after that, but yeah, it was all for science.FLORIDO: Sure. All for science.
contentParagraphs.zh: 主持人 ADRIAN FLORIDO：昆虫学家 Akito Kawahara 正在墨西哥度假，突然他对更多知识产生了渴望。AKITO KAWAHARA：我们刚刚点了一些当地的梅斯卡尔酒和龙舌兰酒。当我们开始喝饮料时，我们意识到，你知道，玻璃杯底部有一条小虫子。我们说，哦，我想知道这是什么。 佛罗里达：昆虫学家研究昆虫及其与环境和人类的关系，主要是在它们的栖息地而不是小酒馆里追捕令人毛骨悚然的爬虫。但科学发生在科学发生的地方。 川原：我们从来没有进行过这样的项目，其中我们已经将蠕虫保存在瓶子里。 弗洛里多：川原是佛罗里达自然历史博物馆的研究员，该博物馆隶属于盖恩斯维尔的佛罗里达大学。他想知道他的饮料里有哪种蠕虫——乍一看并不容易，因为酒精会改变它们的外观。所以当然，他必须带一堆瓶子回家。 KAWAHARA：我们得到了所有这些瓶子。我们把他们排成一排。我们给所有瓶子拍了照片。我们从每个瓶子里取出标本，然后拍了照片。所以我们有每个标本的数字图像。我们必须取出一小块组织，然后将其旋转下来。我们粉碎它。我们从组织本身中分离出 DNA。 弗洛里多： 川原和他的团队使用 DNA 测序来识别这些蠕虫，它们实际上是蛾幼虫。 川原： 这个物种是 Comadia redtenbacheri，即龙舌兰红虫蛾。 弗洛里多： 龙舌兰红虫蛾生活在瓦哈卡州的一种特定类型的龙舌兰植物上。在回程时，川原追踪到了这些特定蠕虫的来源地龙舌兰田。事实证明，为了收集这些蠕虫并将它们放入一瓶梅斯卡尔酒中，生产者不仅仅是将它们从植物表面拔下来。他们必须把它们挖出来，经常杀死龙舌兰植物本身。人们担心，随着对梅斯卡尔和梅斯卡尔蠕虫的需求增长，这种蠕虫及其赖以生存的已经濒临灭绝的龙舌兰植物可能会面临进一步的风险。 河原：所以这些是我们需要提出的研究问题，特别是在许多龙舌兰植物正在消失，并且可能与它们相关的昆虫也正在消失的时候。 弗洛里多：识别这些蠕虫 - 试图找出更可持续的方法的第一步收获它们的方法——一个可能具有重大意义的小发现，值得干杯。 河原：（笑声）是的。之后我们举办了很多聚会，但是，是的，这都是为了科学。 弗洛里多：当然。一切为了科学。

articles.title: How the world failed Ryan and Yaman
articles.summary: An Israeli strike killed my children. What pains me is not just their loss but also the normalisation of their murder.
articles.titleZh: 世界如何辜负了瑞安和亚曼
articles.summaryZh: 以色列的一次袭击杀死了我的孩子们。让我痛苦的不仅仅是他们的离去，还有他们被谋杀的常态化。

contentParagraphs.en: I woke up beneath the rubble, surrounded by darkness, dust, collapsed concrete and the screams of my six-year-old son Nasser crying hysterically above the ruins, trying to reach my buried fingers.In those moments, I believed I was dying.What I did not yet know was that a part of me had already died.When I emerged, I discovered that my 51-day-old baby Ryan had been recovered lifeless after spending more than an hour trapped under the debris. He was a child born during a temporary “ceasefire” in the war. Life had briefly granted him permission to see the world before taking him away almost immediately.His body was so small that I wrapped him in part of my own clothing, afraid he would feel cold.I was told Yaman, my seven-year-old, had suffered only minor injuries and had been taken to the hospital. The truth, however, was that my little boy had died before reaching it. They brought him back to me lifeless, only moments after I had bid farewell to Ryan.On that winter day in January 2024 on the outskirts of Gaza City, my whole world was shattered.Like countless mothers in Gaza, I had feared hunger for my children. I had feared displacement, terror and interrupted education. But despite everything, I never dared to think of death.Ryan never had the chance to grow up and enjoy his childhood. He was denied the chance to run, play and laugh with his brothers.Yaman, on the other hand, had shown us his amazing potential.We called him “the little philosopher” because of the way he spoke formal Arabic with astonishing fluency and spent hours watching documentaries about space, wildlife, oceans and plants. He loved books deeply, memorised stories of the prophets and joined a Quran memorisation centre shortly before the war. Even during bombardment and displacement, we continued reciting verses together.The author’s son Yaman in his school graduation gown. [Courtesy of Aya Shamaa]He was a very sensitive child. He refused to eat meat because he loved animals so much and could not understand why they were harmed and killed.After our home was partially destroyed early in the war, I remember feeling devastated. Yaman came to comfort me with the confidence only children possess and said, “Mama, don’t be sad. After the war, I’ll build you a bigger and more beautiful house.”In Gaza, the genocide is not just the mass killing of children. It is erasing human potential, destroying bright futures. It is taking away the scientist who could have discovered a cure for a deadly disease, the writer who could have written an award-winning book, the engineer who could have devised a new invention to help humanity, the son who could have built his mother a big, beautiful house.And perhaps what is even crueller than death itself is how ordinary loss has become in Gaza. For the rest of the world, Ryan and Yaman were just two entries added to the statistic of 21,000 Palestinian children massacred. Nameless and faceless for the world, they were everything for us.My surviving son, Nasser, became an only child after losing both his brothers. I still remember him pulling at Yaman’s white burial shroud, crying and refusing to let them take his brother away. Since that day, he has never been the same. He spends long hours staring silently at photographs of Yaman on a mobile phone as though he is trying to understand how a child can disappear so suddenly.This war does not leave only corpses beneath the rubble. It leaves survivors buried beneath psychological ruins that crush their souls day by day.Today is International Children’s Day, a day dedicated to children’s rights and wellbeing. For me, it is a day to reflect on how the world failed to protect my children.This is a world that has three other “children’s days”: World Children’s Day, the International Day of the Boy Child and the International Day of the Girl Child. It has a Convention on the Rights of the Child. It has national and international laws protecting children. It has a special United Nations agency dedicated to children, UNICEF. It has countless organisations dedicated to protecting children, feeding them, educating them, providing healthcare for them, etc.Why have all of these special days, organisations and laws when they do nothing to stop the massacres of children?Ryan and Yaman were taken away from me in January 2024. Thousands of other Palestinian mothers have had to bury their children since then. There is a “ceasefire” now, and children are still being killed on an almost daily basis in Gaza.Why have images of children wrapped in white shrouds become so easily normalised? Why has the world witnessed this scale of slaughter and not collapsed morally under its weight?Perhaps because the world has grown accustomed to seeing Palestinian children as numbers, not as human beings. Perhaps because decades of dehumanisation have finally borne fruit.But behind every number, there is a mother’s eternal love.Behind every number, there is a mother who still remembers the sound of her child’s voice, the foods he refused to eat, the dreams he spoke about and the tiny details life never allowed him enough time to enjoy.There is me: the mother who still remembers the soft cry of her baby boy Ryan and the soft-spoken voice of seven-year-old Yaman.Ryan and Yaman are not numbers. They are my children whom the world failed to protect.
contentParagraphs.zh: 我在废墟下醒来，周围是黑暗、灰尘、倒塌的混凝土，还有我六岁的儿子纳赛尔在废墟上歇斯底里地哭泣，试图够到我埋在地下的手指的尖叫声。在那一刻，我相信我快死了。我还不知道的是，我的一部分已经死了。当我出来时，我发现我 51 天大的婴儿瑞恩在被困在废墟下一个多小时后已经毫无生气。他是在战争暂时“停火”期间出生的孩子。生活短暂地允许他见识这个世界，然后几乎立即把他带走了。他的身体太小了，我用自己的衣服把他包裹起来，担心他会感到冷。有人告诉我，我七岁的孩子雅曼只受了轻伤，已被送往医院。然而事实是，我的小男孩在到达目的地之前就已经死了。就在我告别瑞安之后不久，他们把他带回了我身边，已经毫无生气。2024 年 1 月的那个冬日，在加沙城郊区，我的整个世界都破碎了。像加沙无数的母亲一样，我担心我的孩子会挨饿。我担心流离失所、恐怖和教育中断。但尽管如此，我从来不敢想到死亡。瑞恩从来没有机会长大并享受他的童年。他被剥夺了与兄弟们一起奔跑、玩耍和欢笑的机会。另一方面，亚曼向我们展示了他惊人的潜力。我们称他为“小哲学家”，因为他流利地说着正式的阿拉伯语，并花几个小时观看有关太空、野生动物、海洋和植物的纪录片。他热爱书籍，熟记先知的故事，并在战前不久加入了古兰经背诵中心。即使在轰炸和流离失所期间，我们仍继续一起背诵经文。作者的儿子亚曼穿着学校的毕业礼服。 [Aya Shamaa 提供]他是一个非常敏感的孩子。他拒绝吃肉，因为他非常喜欢动物，不明白为什么它们会受到伤害和杀害。我们的家在战争初期被部分摧毁后，我记得感到非常沮丧。亚曼用只有孩子才有的自信来安慰我，他说：“妈妈，别伤心。战争结束后，我会给你建造一座更大、更漂亮的房子。” 在加沙，种族灭绝不仅仅是对儿童的大规模屠杀。它正在抹杀人类的潜力，摧毁光明的未来。它夺走了一个本可以找到治疗致命疾病的方法的科学家，一个本可以写出一本获奖书籍的作家，一个本可以设计出一项新发明来帮助人类的工程师，一个本可以为母亲建造一座又大又漂亮的房子的儿子。也许比死亡本身更残酷的是加沙的普通损失。对于世界其他地方来说，瑞安和亚曼只是被屠杀的 21,000 名巴勒斯坦儿童统计数据中的两个条目。对于这个世界来说，他们无名无名，但他们却是我们的一切。我幸存的儿子纳赛尔在失去了两个兄弟后成为独生子。我仍然记得他拉着亚曼的白色裹尸布，哭泣着，拒绝让他们带走他的兄弟。从那天起，他就不再一样了。他花了很长时间默默地盯着手机上亚曼的照片，仿佛在试图理解一个孩子怎么会突然消失。这场战争在瓦砾下留下的不仅仅是尸体。它让幸存者被埋在心理废墟之下，日复一日地摧残着他们的灵魂。今天是国际儿童节，这一天致力于儿童权利和福祉。对我来说，这一天是反思世界如何未能保护我的孩子的一天。这个世界还有另外三个“儿童节”：世界儿童日、国际男孩日和国际女童日。它有一项《儿童权利公约》。它有保护儿童的国家和国际法律。它有一个专门致力于儿童事务的联合国机构：联合国儿童基金会。它有无数的组织致力于保护儿童、喂养儿童、教育儿童、为他们提供医疗保健等。为什么有这些特殊的日子、组织和法律却没有采取任何措施来制止对儿童的屠杀？瑞安和亚曼于 2024 年 1 月从我身边被带走。从那时起，成千上万的其他巴勒斯坦母亲不得不埋葬她们的孩子。现在已经“停火”了，加沙几乎每天都有儿童被杀害。为什么裹着白色裹尸布的儿童形象如此容易被常态化呢？为什么世界目睹了如此大规模的屠杀，却没有在其重压下道德崩溃？也许是因为世界已经习惯于将巴勒斯坦儿童视为数字，而不是人类。或许是因为数十年的非人化终于结出了硕果。但每一个数字的背后，都有一位母亲永恒的爱。每一个数字的背后，都有一位母亲还记得孩子的声音、他拒绝吃的食物、他所说的梦想以及生活不允许他有足够时间享受的细节。还有我：母亲还记得她的男婴瑞安轻柔的哭声和七岁孩子亚曼轻声细语的声音。瑞安和亚曼不是数字。他们是我的孩子，世界未能保护他们。

articles.title: Charities decry UK plan to use AI to assess age of young asylum seekers
articles.summary:
Coalition of more than 100 organisations says move could lead to more children ending up in adult detention facilities
A coalition of more than a hundred refugee children’s organisations has said controversial plans to use AI to assess the age of young asylum seekers could lead t
articles.titleZh: 慈善机构谴责英国计划使用人工智能来评估年轻寻求庇护者的年龄
articles.summaryZh:
100多个组织组成的联盟表示此举可能导致更多儿童最终被关进成人拘留设施
由一百多个难民儿童组织组成的联盟表示，利用人工智能评估年轻寻求庇护者年龄的有争议的计划可能会导致

contentParagraphs.en: A coalition of more than a hundred refugee children’s organisations has said controversial plans to use AI to assess the age of young asylum seekers could lead to more children wrongly ending up in adult prisons or detention centres.The warning follows a Home Office announcement on Friday of a contract to roll out AI facial age estimation technology on young asylum seekers whose age is disputed.A report from the Refugee and Migrant Children’s Consortium seen by the Guardian before its release in June raises the alarm about the risks of using such technology on young people who do not fit the norms of others in their age group.The consortium, whose member organisations work to promote and protect the rights of refugee and migrant children, say that as a result of trauma, under-nutrition and the harrowing journeys the young people have undertaken to reach safety means AI assessment is complex.The report – Benchmarks and Borders: the use of facial age estimation to assess the age of unaccompanied young people seeking asylum – does not rule out the use of AI altogether, but cautions against relying on it and says it should not be a substitute for comprehensive age assessments carried out by social workers.It urges the Home Office to use AI in an advisory rather than determinative capacity, with a range of safeguards built in including access to an appropriate adult, legal advice and a right to challenge decisions.It urges government not to replace the human errors made in some age–assessment cases with machine error.Age assessments of this group of young people is complex, especially as the majority of lone child asylum seekers coming to the UK are aged 16 or 17. According to Home Office data, young asylum seekers are more than twice as likely to be recorded as children in assessments by social workers than those carried out by immigration officers at the border, with more than two-thirds assessed to be minors.The Home Office’s announcement places significant emphasis on adults making “fake claims”, pretending to be children and attempting to “game the system”, but it does also acknowledge the need to safeguard minors.Two children are accompanied up a beach in Gravelines, France, after failing to board a dinghy attempting to cross the Channel in April. Photograph: Tom Nicholson/Getty ImagesThe minister for border security and asylum, Alex Norris, said: “For too long, adult migrants making false age claims have exploited the system and diverted vital support away from children at risk.“That is why we are rolling out AI technology to put a stop to this, ensuring those who game the system are identified, detained and removed without delay, and those who deserve support and protection are given it.”Final decisions will continue to be made by immigration officers, and the Home Office said the technology would undergo rigorous testing, evaluation and assurance before national rollout.The co-chair of the Refugee and Migrant Children’s Consortium, Kamena Dorling, said: “The government’s proposals are deeply concerning. AI cannot account for the factors that can significantly affect a young person’s appearance after fleeing conflict and persecution and undertaking dangerous journeys, including trauma, malnutrition, and exhaustion.“Existing evidence also shows that AI faces the same problems with bias and inaccuracy as human decision-making, with similar patterns of errors.”Kama Petruczenko, a senior policy analyst at the Refugee Council and member of the consortium, said: “The government’s own figures already show that hundreds of children are being wrongly treated as adults following flawed visual assessments at the border, with devastating consequences for their safety and wellbeing.“AI and facial age estimation technology are not a simple or risk-free answer to these longstanding problems. Poor image quality and bias in datasets can also affect accuracy.“There is a real danger that this technology creates a false sense of certainty in decisions that are already extremely difficult to get right. If flawed assessments are simply automated, more children could end up wrongly placed in adult accommodation, detention centres or even prisons.”The Home Office says AI will estimate an individual’s age in seconds by analysing facial photographs already taken of small-boat arrivals at Dover. A contract for the work worth £322,000 over three years has been awarded to Akhter Computers Ltd, under which the technology will be further tested and developed before being rolled out in 2027.
contentParagraphs.zh: 由一百多个难民儿童组织组成的联盟表示，利用人工智能评估年轻寻求庇护者年龄的计划颇具争议，可能会导致更多儿童被错误地关进成人监狱或拘留中心。在此警告之前，内政部周五宣布签订一份合同，为年龄有争议的年轻寻求庇护者推出人工智能面部年龄估计技术。英国《卫报》在 6 月份发布前看到的难民和移民儿童联盟的一份报告，对这一风险敲响了警钟。该联盟的成员组织致力于促进和保护难民和移民儿童的权利，该联盟表示，由于创伤、营养不良以及年轻人为达到安全而经历的痛苦旅程，人工智能评估变得复杂。该报告《基准与边界：使用面部年龄估计来评估寻求庇护的无人陪伴年轻人的年龄》并不排除使用人工智能总体而言，但警告不要依赖它，并表示它不应该取代社会工作者进行的全面年龄评估。它敦促内政部以咨询而非决定性的能力使用人工智能，并内置一系列保障措施，包括接触适当的成年人、法律建议和质疑决定的权利。它敦促政府不要用机器错误取代某些年龄评估案件中的人为错误。对这群年轻人的年龄评估很复杂，特别是因为大多数是独生子女来到英国的寻求庇护者年龄为 16 岁或 17 岁。根据内政部的数据，年轻的寻求庇护者在社会工作者的评估中被记录为儿童的可能性是边境移民官员进行的评估的两倍多，其中三分之二以上被评估为未成年人。内政部的声明非常强调成年人提出“虚假索赔”、假装儿童和试图“玩弄系统”，但它也承认有必要保护未成年人。四月份，孩子们在未能登上试图穿越英吉利海峡的小艇后，在法国格拉沃利讷的一个海滩上受到陪伴。边境安全和庇护部长亚历克斯·诺里斯 (Alex Norris) 表示：“长期以来，虚假年龄申报的成年移民利用该系统，转移了对处于危险中的儿童的重要支持。这就是为什么我们正在推出人工智能技术来制止这种情况，确保毫不拖延地识别、拘留和驱逐那些玩弄系统的人，并为那些应该得到支持和保护的人提供支持和保护。”最终决定将继续由移民官员做出，内政部表示该技术在全国推广之前将经过严格的测试、评估和保证。难民和移民儿童联合会联合主席卡梅纳·多林表示：“政府的提议令人深感担忧。人工智能无法解释那些在逃离冲突和迫害并经历危险旅程后会严重影响年轻人外貌的因素，包括创伤、营养不良和疲惫。“现有证据还表明，人工智能面临着与人类决策相同的偏见和不准确问题，以及类似的错误模式。”难民委员会高级政策分析师、该联盟成员卡马·彼得鲁岑科(Kama Petruczenko)表示：“政府自己的数据已经显示，数百名儿童在边境进行有缺陷的视觉评估后，他们被错误地视为成年人，这对他们的安全和福祉造成了毁灭性的后果。“人工智能和面部年龄估计技术并不是解决这些长期存在的问题的简单或无风险的答案。图像质量差和数据集中的偏差也会影响准确性。“这项技术存在一个真正的危险，那就是在决策中产生一种错误的确定感，而这些决策本来就很难做出正确的决定。如果有缺陷的评估只是自动化，更多的儿童可能最终被错误地安置在成人宿舍、拘留中心甚至监狱中。”内政部表示，人工智能将通过分析抵达多佛的小船拍摄的面部照片，在几秒钟内估算出一个人的年龄。 Akhter Computers Ltd 已获得一份为期三年、价值 322,000 英镑的合同，根据该合同，该技术将得到进一步测试和开发，然后于 2027 年推出。

articles.title: Abusive passengers could be blacklisted from all airlines under new proposal
articles.summary: The scheme would allow airlines to share information on disruptive passengers and potentially restrict their access to flights.
articles.titleZh: 根据新提案，所有航空公司都可能将虐待乘客列入黑名单
articles.summaryZh: 该计划将允许航空公司分享有关扰乱乘客的信息，并可能限制他们乘坐航班。

articles.title: Experimental pill promises new hope for deadly pancreatic cancer
articles.summary: A novel pill helped people with advanced pancreatic cancer live longer, researchers reported Sunday, raising hopes of long-needed better treatments for one of the deadliest types of cancer.
articles.titleZh: 实验性药物为致命胰腺癌带来新希望
articles.summaryZh: 研究人员周日报道称，一种新型药物可以帮助晚期胰腺癌患者延长寿命，这为这种最致命的癌症之一带来了长期需要的更好治疗方法的希望。

contentParagraphs.en: This undated microscope image from USC via the NIH shows pancreatic cancer cells, nuclei in blue, growing as a sphere encased in membranes, red.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这张未注明日期的显微镜图像显示，胰腺癌细胞（蓝色的细胞核）生长为包裹在红色膜中的球体。

contentParagraphs.en: WASHINGTON — A novel pill helped people with advanced pancreatic cancer live longer, researchers reported Sunday, raising hopes of long-needed better treatments for one of the deadliest types of cancer.
contentParagraphs.zh: 华盛顿——研究人员周日报道称，一种新型药物可以帮助晚期胰腺癌患者延长寿命，这为人们对这种最致命的癌症之一提供长期需要的更好治疗方法带来了希望。

contentParagraphs.en: "While not curing the cancer, it is a very large step forward," said Dr. Zev Wainberg, of the University of California, Los Angeles, who helped lead the study.
contentParagraphs.zh: “虽然不能治愈癌症，但这是向前迈出的一大步，”帮助领导这项研究的加州大学洛杉矶分校的 Zev Wainberg 博士说。

contentParagraphs.en: The drug is called daraxonrasib and it blocks a mutated protein that fuels tumor growth in more than 90% of pancreatic cancer cases — a target that had eluded treatment for decades.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这种药物名为 daraxonrasib，它能阻断一种突变蛋白，这种蛋白在 90% 以上的胰腺癌病例中会促进肿瘤生长，而这一靶点几十年来一直无法得到治疗。

contentParagraphs.en: The daily pills nearly doubled survival time, with fewer severe side effects, in a study that randomly assigned the experimental drug or more chemotherapy to 500 patients whose metastatic, or spreading, cancer had quit responding to prior treatment. The findings were published in the New England Journal of Medicine and presented Sunday at the American Society for Clinical Oncology meeting in Chicago.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一项研究将实验药物或更多的化疗随机分配给 500 名转移性或扩散性癌症对先前治疗已不再有反应的患者，每日服用的药物几乎使生存时间延长了一倍，而且严重副作用也更少。研究结果发表在《新英格兰医学杂志》上，并于周日在芝加哥举行的美国临床肿瘤学会会议上公布。

contentParagraphs.en: Those taking daraxonrasib lived for a median of 13.2 months compared with 6.7 months for chemotherapy recipients. While that may seem like a small improvement, Wainberg said it marked the first drug to show a substantial advantage over chemotherapy.
contentParagraphs.zh: 服用 daraxonrasib 的患者的中位生存期为 13.2 个月，而化疗接受者的中位生存期为 6.7 个月。虽然这看起来似乎是一个小小的进步，但温伯格表示，这标志着第一种比化疗显示出显着优势的药物。

contentParagraphs.en: "Having treated pancreatic cancer for 16 years, I actually started crying" when first seeing the study results, Dr. Rachna Shroff of the University of Arizona Cancer Center, who wasn't involved with the research, said from the ASCO meeting. She was struck by how "patients stayed on this treatment because it was providing durable and meaningful benefit to them."
contentParagraphs.zh: 亚利桑那大学癌症中心的 Rachna Shroff 博士（未参与这项研究）在 ASCO 会议上表示，“我已经治疗胰腺癌 16 年了，当我第一次看到研究结果时，我实际上开始哭泣”。他没有参与这项研究。她对“患者坚持接受这种治疗，因为它为他们提供持久且有意义的益处”感到震惊。

contentParagraphs.en: The pills' effects eventually wane but recipients used them for significantly longer than the comparison group stayed on chemotherapy, reporting less pain and a better quality of life as their tumors shrank. Many still were using the drug after the data was analyzed, which Wainberg said means the survival gap may widen as researchers continue tracking them.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这些药丸的效果最终会减弱，但接受者使用它们的时间明显长于对照组接受化疗的时间，随着肿瘤缩小，他们的疼痛减轻了，生活质量也提高了。数据分析后，许多人仍在使用这种药物，温伯格说，这意味着随着研究人员继续追踪他们，生存差距可能会扩大。

contentParagraphs.en: Dr. Brian Wolpin, of the Dana-Farber Cancer Institute, presented the findings Sunday. He said the drug should become "a new standard of care" for previously treated metastatic pancreatic cancer, adding that researchers also will explore its use earlier in the disease, including to see if tumor shrinkage might let more patients qualify for surgery.
contentParagraphs.zh: 丹纳法伯癌症研究所的 Brian Wolpin 博士周日公布了研究结果。他说，该药物应该成为先前治疗过的转移性胰腺癌的“新护理标准”，并补充说，研究人员还将探索其在疾病早期的使用，包括看看肿瘤缩小是否可以让更多患者有资格接受手术。

contentParagraphs.en: Side effects most likely to affect pill usage were a rash that can be severe and mouth sores, he said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他说，最有可能影响药物使用的副作用是严重的皮疹和口腔溃疡。

contentParagraphs.en: Maker Revolution Medicines funded the study and the Food and Drug Administration plans to expedite review of the drug. Meanwhile, the agency is allowing what's called "expanded access" to the experimental drug for patients who meet certain criteria. The drug garnered public attention when former U.S. Sen. Ben Sasse described on "60 Minutes" how he's had less pain while taking it. Oncologists are being flooded with requests as the special access program gets started.
contentParagraphs.zh: Maker Revolution Medicines 资助了这项研究，美国食品和药物管理局计划加快对该药物的审查。与此同时，该机构允许符合某些标准的患者“扩大使用”实验药物。当前美国参议员本·萨斯 (Ben Sasse) 在“60 分钟”节目中描述他在服用该药物时如何减轻疼痛时，该药物引起了公众的关注。随着特殊准入计划的启动，肿瘤科医生收到了大量的请求。

contentParagraphs.en: Pancreatic cancer is among the most deadly forms in large part because it's hard to detect before it starts spreading to other organs. The American Cancer Society estimates about 67,000 new cases will be diagnosed in the U.S. this year and more than 52,000 people will die from the disease. The five-year overall survival rate is 13%.
contentParagraphs.zh: 胰腺癌是最致命的癌症之一，很大程度上是因为在它开始扩散到其他器官之前很难被发现。美国癌症协会估计，今年美国将诊断出约 67,000 例新病例，超过 52,000 人将死于这种疾病。五年总生存率为13%。

contentParagraphs.en: Unlike with other cancers that have benefitted from a variety of chemotherapy alternatives, pancreatic cancer has been harder to tackle.
contentParagraphs.zh: 与受益于多种化疗方案的其他癌症不同，胰腺癌更难治疗。

contentParagraphs.en: Cancer specialists not involved in the new research expressed optimism that this may be a turning point in the quest for new options, with dozens of experimental drugs in development.
contentParagraphs.zh: 未参与这项新研究的癌症专家乐观地认为，这可能是寻求新选择的一个转折点，目前有数十种实验药物正在开发中。

contentParagraphs.en: The new drug targets mutations in the RAS gene family that normally regulates cell growth. So-called KRAS mutations are especially critical in fueling pancreatic cancer. But a structure that made it hard for drugs to stick to the mutated proteins meant this cancer driver was long considered "undruggable."
contentParagraphs.zh: 这种新药针对的是通常调节细胞生长的 RAS 基因家族的突变。所谓的 KRAS 突变对于引发胰腺癌尤其重要。但这种结构使得药物很难粘附在突变的蛋白质上，这意味着这种癌症驱动因素长期以来被认为是“不可成药的”。

contentParagraphs.en: Revolution Medicines' drug uses what's essentially a molecular glue to bind with multiple KRAS subtypes. Wainberg said researchers next will probe whether the drug worked better in certain of those subtypes.
contentParagraphs.zh: Revolution Medicines 的药物本质上使用分子胶来与多种 KRAS 亚型结合。温伯格表示，研究人员接下来将探讨该药物是否对某些亚型更有效。

contentParagraphs.en: The drug will change pancreatic cancer treatment, said Dr. Andrew Coveler of the Fred Hutchinson Cancer Center, who wasn't involved in the research.
contentParagraphs.zh: 弗雷德·哈钦森癌症中心的安德鲁·科维勒博士（未参与这项研究）表示，这种药物将改变胰腺癌的治疗。

contentParagraphs.en: "This thing works drastically differently," he said.
contentParagraphs.zh: “这件事的运作方式截然不同，”他说。

contentParagraphs.en: Wainberg said other drugs in development target specific KRAS subtypes. Other approaches in earlier stages of testing include vaccines designed to prevent recurrence after pancreatic cancer surgery by teaching the immune system to recognize the mutated protein.
contentParagraphs.zh: Wainberg 说，正在开发的其他药物针对特定的 KRAS 亚型。早期测试阶段的其他方法包括通过教导免疫系统识别突变蛋白来预防胰腺癌手术后复发的疫苗。

articles.title: Ukraine’s forcibly transferred children must not be a bargaining chip
articles.summary: Russia must face more pressure to fulfil its legal obligations to return the Ukrainian children.
articles.titleZh: 乌克兰强行转移儿童绝不能成为讨价还价的筹码
articles.summaryZh: 俄罗斯必须面临更大压力，以履行遣返乌克兰儿童的法律义务。

contentParagraphs.en: It has been more than four years since Russia launched a full-scale invasion of Ukraine, expanding its occupation of Ukrainian lands, which started in 2014. In the chaos and violence of the first months of the invasion, families were separated, and childcare institutions were cut off from the control of the central authorities in Kyiv. As a result, the occupation forces forcibly transferred more than 20,000 Ukrainian children to Russia.Russian officials claimed that they did not abduct Ukrainian children, but “saved” them through humanitarian evacuations. However, international investigations have since found that many such transfers were unlawful under international humanitarian law. In many documented cases, transfers were carried out without the consent of the living parent or legal guardians of the child.International humanitarian law prohibits all forcible transfers and deportations of protected people from occupied territory, except for evacuations strictly required to ensure the population’s safety. Even then, evacuation must happen within occupied territory, be temporary, preserve family unity and return evacuees home as soon as hostilities cease.Today, the lives of thousands of Ukrainian children are devastated by this forcible transfer. Instead of abiding by international legal obligations and returning them to their homeland, Russia has transformed the issue into yet another bargaining chip against the Ukrainian people.But Ukraine refuses to abandon its children. For the past four years, there have been intense efforts from families, NGOs and the Ukrainian government to bring them back.Take the case of Lesya (the name has been changed to protect her identity), whose testimony was recorded by The Reckoning Project— a global team of journalists and lawyers documenting and publicising atrocities committed in the war. Lesya was 15 years old when Russian forces occupied her village in the Kherson region in 2022. When the occupation authorities imposed a mandatory evacuation, she was put on a truck with more than 30 other children and was sent to a rehabilitation centre in Feodosia, Crimea. A woman accompanying the children told her that her mother would join her shortly.At the facility, Lesya and other Ukrainian children were subjected to a strict routine, forced to do chores and study in Russian, using Russian textbooks. They were kept under surveillance indoors most of the time in a building with windows that could not be opened. Two days a week, the children underwent military training.Eventually, a relative located her, and with the help of Save Ukraine, a Ukrainian NGO facilitating children’s return, her mother managed to bring her back.But Lesya’s case is the exception rather than the rule. More than 2,000 Ukrainian children have been brought back thanks to efforts by NGOs, the government and foreign mediators.Pressure through international institutions has also been pursued, but that has not accelerated the process of return.In March 2023, the International Criminal Court issued warrants of arrest for Russian President Vladimir Putin and Commissioner for Children’s Rights Maria Lvova-Belova for the unlawful deportation and transfer of Ukrainian children.In July 2025, the European Court of Human Rights, in Ukraine and the Netherlands v Russia, found Russia responsible for a number of human rights violations, including the organised removal of children. The court also required Russia to cooperate in establishing a mechanism to find and safely return children.In March this year, the United Nations Independent International Commission of Inquiry on Ukraine concluded that Russia’s deportation and forcible transfer of Ukrainian children amount to crimes against humanity. The report identifies the removal of Ukrainian children as a part of a well-planned and systematically executed policy, conceived at the highest level.On May 11, the European Union sanctioned 16 individuals and seven entities, while the United Kingdom sanctioned 29 individuals and entities responsible for the deportation, forced transfer, forced assimilation, indoctrination, militarisation and unlawful adoption of Ukrainian children. Overall, the EU has sanctioned more than 130 people and organisations for these actions. The United States, Canada, Australia, Japan, Switzerland and several other countries have introduced similar measures.The lack of progress on this issue has driven families to desperation. Some have tried to bring their children back on their own or through often-daring missions by Save Ukraine and five other Ukrainian NGOs.There should be no need for these risky missions. Under international humanitarian law, Russia is obligated to identify and register Ukrainian children in their care, facilitate family reunification, and permit access to neutral actors assisting Ukrainian children.As negotiations for the end of the war have stalled and other global events have displaced Ukraine from global headlines, we urgently need to put the issue of the abducted Ukrainian children back in the spotlight.There are several areas in which existing efforts can expand.First, a comprehensive tracing mechanism needs to be established and financed to track abducted Ukrainian children and prevent their disappearance into dispersed care and adoption systems.Second, ongoing legal efforts to hold to account Russian officials involved in the abduction should be intensified. This means coordinated prosecutions in states where the universal jurisdiction principle can be applied, as well as joint investigation strategies supported by Eurojust, the EU’s judicial hub. Ukraine’s partners should support its judicial processes launched against Russian officials and cooperate where needed, including through extraditions where legally applicable and other lawful transfer mechanisms. While justice may be slow, the prospect of accountability can have a deterrent effect.Third, states can and should fully implement sanctions, trade re
contentParagraphs.zh: 俄罗斯自2014年开始全面入侵乌克兰，扩大对乌克兰领土的占领，至今已有四年多了。在入侵头几个月的混乱和暴力中，许多家庭被离散，儿童保育机构也脱离了基辅中央当局的控制。结果，占领军强行将2万多名乌克兰儿童转移到俄罗斯。俄罗斯官员声称，他们没有绑架乌克兰儿童，而是通过人道主义疏散“拯救”了他们。然而，国际调查发现，根据国际人道法，许多此类转移都是非法的。在许多记录在案的案例中，转移是在没有得到儿童在世父母或法定监护人同意的情况下进行的。国际人道法禁止对受保护人员从被占领土进行任何强行转移和驱逐，但为确保民众安全而严格要求的撤离除外。即便如此，撤离也必须在被占领土内进行，而且是暂时的，以维护家庭团聚，并在敌对行动停止后立即将撤离者送回家。如今，数千名乌克兰儿童的生活因这种强行转移而遭到破坏。俄罗斯非但没有遵守国际法律义务，将他们送回祖国，反而把这个问题变成了针对乌克兰人民的又一个筹码。但乌克兰拒绝抛弃自己的孩子。在过去的四年里，家人、非政府组织和乌克兰政府为将他们带回来做出了巨大的努力。以 Lesya 为例（为了保护她的身份，化名），她的证词由清算项目记录，该项目是一个由记者和律师组成的全球团队，记录和宣传战争中犯下的暴行。 2022 年，俄罗斯军队占领赫尔松地区的 Lesya 村庄时，她只有 15 岁。占领当局强制疏散时，她与其他 30 多名儿童一起被抬上一辆卡车，送往克里米亚费奥多西亚的康复中心。一名陪伴孩子们的妇女告诉她，她的母亲很快就会来接她。 在该设施中，莱西娅和其他乌克兰儿童受到严格的训练，被迫做家务并使用俄语教科书用俄语学习。他们大部分时间都在室内受到监视，所在的建筑物窗户无法打开。孩子们每周有两天接受军事训练。最终，一位亲戚找到了她，在乌克兰非政府组织“拯救乌克兰”的帮助下，她的母亲设法将她带了回来。但莱西亚的情况是例外，而不是常规。在非政府组织、政府和外国调解人的努力下，2000多名乌克兰儿童已被带回国。国际机构也不断施压，但这并没有加速返回进程。2023年3月，国际刑事法院因非法驱逐和转移乌克兰儿童而对俄罗斯总统弗拉基米尔·普京和儿童权利专员玛丽亚·利沃娃-贝洛娃发出逮捕令。 2025年，欧洲人权法院在乌克兰和荷兰诉俄罗斯一案中认定俄罗斯应对一系列侵犯人权行为负责，包括有组织地驱逐儿童。法院还要求俄罗斯合作建立寻找并安全遣返儿童的机制。 今年3月，联合国乌克兰问题独立国际调查委员会得出结论，俄罗斯驱逐和强行转移乌克兰儿童的行为构成反人类罪。报告指出，驱逐乌克兰儿童是最高层精心策划和系统执行政策的一部分。5月11日，欧盟制裁了16名个人和7个实体，英国则制裁了29名对乌克兰儿童进行驱逐、强制转移、强制同化、洗脑、军事化和非法收养负有责任的个人和实体。总体而言，欧盟已因这些行为制裁了 130 多个个人和组织。美国、加拿大、澳大利亚、日本、瑞士等几个国家也出台了类似措施。这一问题缺乏进展，让家庭陷入绝望。有些人试图靠自己或通过“拯救乌克兰”和其他五个乌克兰非政府组织经常进行的大胆任务来带回自己的孩子。应该没有必要进行这些冒险的任务。根据国际人道法，俄罗斯有义务对受其照顾的乌克兰儿童进行身份识别和登记，促进家庭团聚，并允许援助乌克兰儿童的中立行为体进入。由于结束战争的谈判陷入僵局，而其他全球事件使乌克兰不再成为全球头条新闻，我们迫切需要将被绑架的乌克兰儿童问题重新置于聚光灯下。现有的努力可以在几个领域进行扩展。首先，需要建立一个全面的寻人机制并为其提供资金以进行追踪绑架乌克兰儿童并防止他们在分散的看护和收养系统中失踪。其次，应加强正在进行的法律努力，追究参与绑架的俄罗斯官员的责任。这意味着在可以适用普遍管辖权原则的国家进行协调起诉，以及欧盟司法中心欧洲司法组织支持的联合调查策略。乌克兰的合作伙伴应支持针对俄罗斯官员启动的司法程序，并在必要时进行合作，包括通过合法适用的引渡和其他合法的移交机制。虽然正义可能会缓慢，但问责的前景可能会产生威慑作用。第三，各国可以而且应该全面实施制裁、贸易制裁

articles.title: Meta legal action forces Facebook whistleblower to sit in silence at Hay festival
articles.summary:
Sarah Wynn-Williams did not speak during event after lawyers warned of possible sanctions from tech firm
Facebook whistleblower Sarah Wynn-Williams was forced to sit in silence on stage at an event at Hay festival, after lawyers advised her not to speak because of ongoing legal a
articles.titleZh: 元法律行动迫使 Facebook 举报人在 Hay 节上保持沉默
articles.summaryZh:
在律师警告科技公司可能实施制裁后，莎拉·韦恩·威廉姆斯在活动中没有发言
Facebook 举报人莎拉·韦恩·威廉姆斯 (Sarah Wynn-Williams) 被迫在海伊音乐节 (Hay Festival) 的一次活动中保持沉默，因为律师建议她不要发言，因为法律仍在继续。

contentParagraphs.en: Facebook whistleblower Sarah Wynn-Williams was forced to sit in silence on stage at an event at Hay festival, after lawyers advised her not to speak because of ongoing legal action brought by Meta.Wynn-Williams, whose bestselling memoir, Careless People, details her years working at Facebook, was due to appear in conversation with the investigative journalist Carole Cadwalladr and academic Tim Wu.Instead, Wynn-Williams sat on stage for the duration of the hour-long discussion between Cadwalladr and Wu, without speaking or responding. She was unable even to nod or shake her head.Introducing the panel, Cadwalladr said: “I think this might be a Hay first, in which we have an author in a hostage situation. Blink once if you can hear us, Sarah, twice if [Mark] Zuckerberg is an asshole.”At the end of the event, Wynn-Williams received a standing ovation from the audience, during which she was moved to tears.Describing the situation, Cadwalladr said: “I think we can say that Facebook is triggered.”Wynn-Williams, a former Facebook executive, has faced mounting legal restrictions since the publication last year of Careless People, which contains allegations about Meta’s internal culture and decision-making, including claims relating to political influence, the company’s approach to China and concerns about the wellbeing of its child users. Meta has disputed the book’s claims.Hay’s programme director Helen Bagnall told the audience that the moment was “an important act of solidarity for the silenced”.Meta, which owns Facebook and Instagram, secured an emergency legal order on the eve of publication preventing her from publicly discussing aspects of the book, and she faces fines of $50,000 (£37,000) each time she breaches the order. The financial and legal pressure has reportedly threatened her with bankruptcy.Cadwalladr described the spectacle as “trolling-like behaviour” by Meta. “This is not how you conduct crisis comms. Crisis comms would just be simply to ignore this and deprive it of oxygen. This is a kind of trolling-like behaviour against their enemies.”Speaking on stage, Wu condemned the restrictions on Wynn-Williams’ participation.Tim Wu, Sarah Wynn-Williams and Carole Cadwalladr. Wynn-Williams received a standing ovation at the end of the event. Photograph: Sam Hardwick“This is censorship,” he told the audience. “This is a demonstration that some of the worst abuses in our time are not confined to kings, emperors, governments … but to a class of companies that have assumed the sovereign affect, and seek to assert their power the same way that some of those despotic nation states do.”During the event, Cadwalladr read a letter from Wynn-Williams’ lawyers outlining the company’s latest legal claims. The letter stated that, in March 2026, Meta filed a sanctions motion alleging that Wynn-Williams violates the emergency arbitration order “any time she appears in public in a place where she should know that her book is available for sale and her presence might draw attention to it”.According to the letter, Meta’s motion specifically cited her appearance at the Hay festival as “an example of conduct that should be formally sanctioned”.It also referred to the identities of her fellow panellists. Meta argued that Cadwalladr was a journalist “primarily known for her negative coverage of Meta”, while Wu was described as “another known critic”.Following the letter, Hay festival withdrew Careless People from sale while she was speaking at the festival, so as not to breach Meta’s legal order.
contentParagraphs.zh: Facebook 举报人莎拉·韦恩·威廉姆斯 (Sarah Wynn-Williams) 被迫在 Hay 音乐节的一次活动中保持沉默，因为律师建议她不要发言，因为 Meta 正在提起法律诉讼。韦恩·威廉姆斯 (Wynn-Williams) 的畅销回忆录《粗心的人》(Careless People) 详细介绍了她在 Facebook 工作的岁月。 韦恩·威廉姆斯 (Sarah Wynn-Williams) 原本打算与调查记者卡罗尔·卡德瓦拉德尔 (Carole Cadwalladr) 和学者蒂姆·吴 (Tim Wu) 进行对话。卡德瓦拉德尔和吴没有说话，也没有回应。她甚至无法点头或摇头。在介绍小组时，卡德瓦拉德尔说：“我认为这可能是海伊第一，其中有一位作家被劫持为人质。如果你能听到我们的声音，莎拉，请眨眼一次，如果[马克]扎克伯格是个混蛋，请眨眼两次。”活动结束时，韦恩-威廉姆斯赢得了观众的起立鼓掌，期间她感动得流下了眼泪。在描述当时的情况时，卡德瓦拉德尔说： “我认为我们可以说Facebook被触发了。”自去年《Careless People》出版以来，Facebook前高管韦恩-威廉姆斯面临着越来越多的法律限制，其中包含对Meta内部文化和决策的指控，包括与政治影响力、该公司对华政策以及对其儿童用户福祉的担忧有关的指控。 Meta 对这本书的说法提出了质疑。Hay 的节目总监海伦·巴格纳尔 (Helen Bagnall) 告诉观众，这一时刻是“对沉默者的团结的重要举动”。拥有 Facebook 和 Instagram 的 Meta 在出版前夕获得了一项紧急法律命令，禁止她公开讨论这本书的某些方面，每次违反该命令，她都将面临 50,000 美元（37,000 英镑）的罚款。据报道，财务和法律压力使她面临破产的威胁。Cadwalladr 将这一奇观描述为 Meta 的“类似恶搞的行为”。 “这不是进行危机传播的方式。危机传播只是简单地忽视这一点并剥夺其氧气。这是一种针对敌人的恶搞行为。”吴在台上发言时谴责了对永利威廉姆斯参与的限制。蒂姆·吴、莎拉·永利威廉姆斯和卡罗尔·卡德瓦拉德尔。活动结束时，永利威廉姆斯获得了全场起立鼓掌。摄影：萨姆·哈德威克“这就是审查制度，”他告诉观众。 “这表明，我们这个时代一些最严重的侵权行为并不局限于国王、皇帝、政府......而是一类公司，这些公司承担了主权影响，并像一些专制民族国家一样寻求维护自己的权力。”在活动期间，卡德瓦拉德尔宣读了永利威廉姆斯律师的一封信，概述了该公司最新的法律主张。信中称，2026年3月，Meta提出制裁动议，指控永利·威廉姆斯“任何时候在她应该知道她的书可供出售的地方公开露面，并且她的出现可能会引起人们的注意”，都违反了紧急仲裁令。根据信中，Meta的动议特别引用了她出现在海伊音乐节上的行为，称其为“应该受到正式制裁的行为例子”。它还提到了她的其他小组成员的身份。 Meta 辩称，Cadwalladr 是一名记者，“主要因其对 Meta 的负面报道而闻名”，而吴则被描述为“另一位知名批评家”。在这封信之后，Hay 音乐节在她在音乐节上发表讲话时，下架了《Careless People》的销售，以免违反 Meta 的法律秩序。

articles.title: These AI models are free, private, and will never say 'no'
articles.summary: Open-weight AI models with advanced capabilities and no safeguards are becoming much more accessible. While they can be useful, AI safety experts have concerns.
articles.titleZh: 这些人工智能模型是免费的、私密的，并且永远不会说“不”
articles.summaryZh: 具有先进功能且无保障措施的开放式人工智能模型变得更加容易使用。虽然它们很有用，但人工智能安全专家对此表示担忧。

contentParagraphs.en: Participants hold their laptops in front of an illuminated wall at the annual Chaos Computer Club (CCC) computer hackers' congress, called 29C3, on December 28, 2012 in Hamburg, Germany. In 2026, open-weight AI models possess advanced capabilities not far behind their proprietary counterparts. Getting rid of open-weight models' guardrails used to take time and deep expertise. But in recent months, that process has become dramatically more accessible and popular.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2012 年 12 月 28 日在德国汉堡举行的一年一度的混沌计算机俱乐部 (CCC) 计算机黑客大会（名为 29C3）上，与会者将笔记本电脑放在发光墙前。到 2026 年，开放权重 AI 模型拥有的先进功能与专有模型相差不远。过去，摆脱开放重量模型的护栏需要时间和深厚的专业知识。但近几个月来，这一过程变得更加容易获得和流行。

contentParagraphs.en: How do you make explosives using household items? How do you make meth? How do you plan a school shooting? If you ask the popular AI chatbots most people are familiar with, chances are they will say that it's illegal, harmful or that answering would be a policy violation.
contentParagraphs.zh: 如何使用家居用品制作炸药？你如何制作冰毒？你如何计划一次校园枪击事件？如果你问大多数人都熟悉的流行人工智能聊天机器人，他们很可能会说这是非法的、有害的，或者回答会违反政策。

contentParagraphs.en: But another type of AI model will never refuse to provide what the user asks for. In recent months, these models have become more accessible and popular.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但另一种人工智能模型永远不会拒绝提供用户所要求的东西。近几个月来，这些模型变得更加容易获得和流行。

contentParagraphs.en: "Everybody can download and operate their own state-of-the-art model and use it for great things and terrible things," said Noam Schwartz, CEO of Alice, an AI security company that has conducted red-teaming and safety evaluation for AI model developers.
contentParagraphs.zh: “每个人都可以下载并操作自己最先进的模型，并将其用于伟大的事情和可怕的事情，”AI 安全公司 Alice 的首席执行官诺姆·施瓦茨 (Noam Schwartz) 表示，该公司为 AI 模型开发人员进行了红队和安全评估。

contentParagraphs.en: Teaching models when to say "no"
contentParagraphs.zh: 教导何时说“不”

contentParagraphs.en: Big AI companies such as OpenAI, Google, Anthropic and xAI train their proprietary models to refuse requests deemed as harmful or inappropriate. Legions of workers instruct models when and how to refuse certain prompts.
contentParagraphs.zh: OpenAI、Google、Anthropic 和 xAI 等大型人工智能公司训练其专有模型来拒绝被视为有害或不适当的请求。大批工作人员指导模特何时以及如何拒绝某些提示。

contentParagraphs.en: These methods don't always work and carry pitfalls: some harmful requests go through, while other users complain about innocuous requests being refused. Chatbots that initially say "no" can be manipulated into saying "yes" using cleverly phrased prompts, such as posing them as poems. Even with guardrails, popular chatbots have been used to plan mass violence and generate deepfake child sexual abuse material. In some instances, parents have accused AI chatbots of encouraging their children to harm themselves.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这些方法并不总是有效，并且存在陷阱：一些有害的请求会被通过，而其他用户则抱怨无害的请求被拒绝。最初说“不”的聊天机器人可以通过巧妙的措辞提示（例如将它们摆成诗歌）来操纵说“是”。即使有护栏，流行的聊天机器人也被用来策划大规模暴力并生成深度伪造的儿童性虐待材料。在某些情况下，家长指责人工智能聊天机器人鼓励孩子伤害自己。

contentParagraphs.en: But there's a whole other class of AI models whose guardrails are much easier to strip away. They're known as open-weight models. Some are made by tech giants, such as OpenAI and Alibaba, while others are put out by smaller outfits like China's DeepSeek. Like their better-known proprietary counterparts, many possess advanced capabilities such as writing functional code or generating life-like images. Unlike with ChatGPT, Claude or Gemini, it's easier to permanently remove their built-in safety guardrails – and the companies behind them have no idea how they're being used.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但还有一类人工智能模型，其护栏更容易被拆除。它们被称为开放重量模型。其中一些是由 OpenAI 和阿里巴巴等科技巨头开发的，而另一些则是由中国 DeepSeek 等规模较小的公司推出的。与更知名的专有同行一样，许多拥有高级功能，例如编写功能代码或生成栩栩如生的图像。与 ChatGPT、Claude 或 Gemini 不同，永久移除它们内置的安全护栏更容易——而且它们背后的公司不知道它们是如何使用的。

contentParagraphs.en: Getting rid of open-weight models' guardrails used to take time and deep expertise. But in recent months, that process has become dramatically more accessible and popular.
contentParagraphs.zh: 过去，摆脱开放重量模型的护栏需要时间和深厚的专业知识。但近几个月来，这一过程变得更加容易获得和流行。

contentParagraphs.en: Recent method makes removing model guardrails easier than ever
contentParagraphs.zh: 最近的方法使拆除模型护栏比以往更容易

contentParagraphs.en: Safety guardrails of open-weight models can be weakened or removed in many ways. This is largely because the model developers have made what's known as the model weights available to the public. Model weights are sets of parameters, like knobs and dials in a machine, telling the models how to process information.
contentParagraphs.zh: 开放式重量模型的安全护栏可以通过多种方式削弱或移除。这主要是因为模型开发人员向公众公开了所谓的模型权重。模型权重是一组参数，例如机器中的旋钮和转盘，告诉模型如何处理信息。

contentParagraphs.en: One recently developed method called "abliteration" has caught the attention of AI and national security researchers. By tweaking model weights, people can take away the model's ability to say "no."
contentParagraphs.zh: 最近开发的一种名为“消除”的方法引起了人工智能和国家安全研究人员的注意。通过调整模型权重，人们可以消除模型说“不”的能力。

contentParagraphs.en: Hugging Face, which hosts open-source AI models, currently lists over 6,000 abliterated models, compared to about 600 in 2024. On Hugging Face, abliterated models currently outnumber models that have their guardrails removed using other methods, according to research by the National Counterterrorism Innovation, Technology, and Education Center (NCITE), a Department of Homeland Security-supported research consortium based at University of Nebraska at Omaha.
contentParagraphs.zh: 托管开源人工智能模型的 Hugging Face 目前列出了 6,000 多个被删减的模型，而 2024 年大约有 600 个。根据国家反恐创新、技术和教育中心 (NCITE) 的研究，在 Hugging Face 上，被删减的模型目前超过了使用其他方法移除护栏的模型。NCITE 是一个由国土安全部支持的研究联盟，总部设在奥马哈内布拉斯加大学。

contentParagraphs.en: What's more, new tools are making it much easier to create abliterated models. "That was [the job of] the data scientist, you know, a senior employee" at a leading AI lab, said Schwartz. "Now, everybody with access to the internet and a laptop for like 400 bucks can actually run this thing on their own machine."
contentParagraphs.zh: 更重要的是，新工具使创建删减模型变得更加容易。施瓦茨说，“这是数据科学家的工作，你知道，他是一家领先人工智能实验室的高级员工”。 “现在，每个能够访问互联网并拥有 400 美元笔记本电脑的人实际上都可以在自己的机器上运行这个东西。”

contentParagraphs.en: One such tool is Heretic, which automates the abliteration process. All a user has to do to remove a model's guardrails is to give Heretic two lines of instructions, and the process can take as little as a few minutes. The application has gotten more popular on the code repository GitHub since February, according to Alice's research.
contentParagraphs.zh: Heretic 就是这样的工具之一，它可以自动执行消除过程。用户只需向 Heretic 发出两行指令即可移除模型的护栏，整个过程只需几分钟。根据 Alice 的研究，自 2 月份以来，该应用程序在代码存储库 GitHub 上变得越来越受欢迎。

contentParagraphs.en: Some lawmakers are taking notice. In late April, House lawmakers attended a demonstration of abliterated models hosted by NCITE, Politico reported.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一些立法者正在注意到这一点。据 Politico 报道，4 月下旬，众议院议员参加了 NCITE 主办的废除模型示威活动。

contentParagraphs.en: "[What] was frightening about this demonstration was how readily available some of this content or software is on kind of the black market right now, and how it can be weaponized and used to manipulate people, destroy lives and build weapons of mass destruction," said Rep. Andy Ogles (R-TN) in a video put out by Republicans on the House Homeland Security Committee. Hugging Face and GitHub, where abliterated models and the tools to make them can be downloaded, are not black markets.
contentParagraphs.zh: 众议员安迪·奥格尔斯 (Andy Ogles) 在众议院国土安全委员会共和党人发布的视频中表示，“这次示威活动的可怕之处在于，这些内容或软件现在在黑市上很容易获得，以及它们如何被武器化并用于操纵人民、摧毁生命和制造大规模杀伤性武器。” Hugging Face 和 GitHub 并不是黑市，可以下载被删除的模型及其制作工具。

contentParagraphs.en: Models without guardrails can be both useful and dangerous
contentParagraphs.zh: 没有护栏的模型既有用又危险

contentParagraphs.en: It is difficult to get a comprehensive picture of how people are using open-weight models, because these models are run locally on users' computers, and don't need the internet to function. Unlike with proprietary models, the model developers cannot monitor what users are asking the models.
contentParagraphs.zh: 很难全面了解人们如何使用开放权重模型，因为这些模型在用户计算机上本地运行，不需要互联网即可运行。与专有模型不同，模型开发人员无法监控用户对模型的询问。

contentParagraphs.en: But there's growing anecdotal evidence for how people are experimenting with altered models.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但越来越多的轶事证据表明人们正在尝试改变模型。

contentParagraphs.en: Several accounts on X said they have used abliterated models to generate pornography.
contentParagraphs.zh: X 上的几个帐户表示，他们使用删减模型来生成色情内容。

contentParagraphs.en: An individual in a pro-ISIS chat room claimed they used an "uncensored" AI to research the amount and type of explosives needed to destroy "Trump Tower in the U.S.," according to the Counter Extremism Project, a nonprofit that focuses on counterterrorism.
contentParagraphs.zh: 据专注于反恐的非营利组织反极端主义项目称，支持伊斯兰国的聊天室中的一个人声称他们使用“未经审查的”人工智能来研究摧毁“美国特朗普大厦”所需的爆炸物的数量和类型。

contentParagraphs.en: On one cybercrime forum, a user asked for ideas to get around an AI model's guardrails so they could use AI
contentParagraphs.zh: 在一个网络犯罪论坛上，一位用户询问如何绕过人工智能模型的护栏，以便他们可以使用人工智能

articles.title: One by one, U.S. civil rights agency dismantles tools to fight discrimination
articles.summary: The EEOC is seeking to overturn rules created decades ago to tackle discrimination in employment. The Trump administration says those rules have led to more discrimination —against white people.
articles.titleZh: 美国民权机构一一拆除打击歧视的工具
articles.summaryZh: 平等就业机会委员会正在寻求推翻几十年前为解决就业歧视问题制定的规则。特朗普政府表示，这些规则导致了更多针对白人的歧视。

contentParagraphs.en: The EEOC was established by Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 to address entrenched discrimination in employment.
contentParagraphs.zh: 平等就业机会委员会是根据 1964 年《民权法案》第七章设立的，旨在解决就业中根深蒂固的歧视问题。

contentParagraphs.en: Afro American Newspapers/Gado/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国黑人报纸/Gado/Getty Images

contentParagraphs.en: Afro American Newspapers/Gado/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国黑人报纸/Gado/Getty Images

contentParagraphs.en: Stay up to date with our Politics newsletter, sent weekly.
contentParagraphs.zh: 随时了解我们每周发送的政治时事通讯。

contentParagraphs.en: In 1966, the newly-established Equal Employment Opportunity Commission issued a rule to tackle entrenched discrimination on the job.
contentParagraphs.zh: 1966年，新成立的平等就业机会委员会颁布了一项规则，以解决工作中根深蒂固的歧视问题。

contentParagraphs.en: Every year, companies with a hundred or more workers would turn over to the government information about the race, ethnicity, sex and job categories of their employees.
contentParagraphs.zh: 每年，拥有一百名或以上员工的公司都会向政府提交有关员工种族、民族、性别和工作类别的信息。

contentParagraphs.en: This EEO-1 data, as it's known, has helped the federal agency figure out where people of color and women are not getting hired or promoted. Over decades, the EEOC's work has led to settlements worth billions.
contentParagraphs.zh: 众所周知，EEO-1 数据帮助联邦机构找出有色人种和女性在哪些方面没有被雇用或晋升。几十年来，平等就业机会委员会的工作已促成价值数十亿美元的和解。

contentParagraphs.en: Now, as part of a realignment of civil rights enforcement under President Trump, the EEOC is seeking to end its annual data collection while also getting rid of a 1979 regulation that allowed employers to take certain steps to address race and gender imbalances revealed by the data.
contentParagraphs.zh: 现在，作为特朗普总统领导下的民权执法重组的一部分，平等就业机会委员会正在寻求结束其年度数据收集，同时废除 1979 年的一项规定，该规定允许雇主采取某些措施来解决数据所揭示的种族和性别失衡问题。

contentParagraphs.en: Together, the moves would mark an about-face in the civil rights agency's efforts to fulfill its mission.
contentParagraphs.zh: 总之，这些举措将标志着民权机构履行其使命的努力发生转变。

contentParagraphs.en: Andrea Lucas, the Trump-appointed chair of the EEOC, did not respond to NPR's questions about the two proposals, which have been submitted to the White House for review.
contentParagraphs.zh: 特朗普任命的平等就业机会委员会主席安德里亚·卢卡斯没有回应 NPR 关于这两项提案的问题，这两项提案已提交白宫审查。

contentParagraphs.en: But in interviews and public remarks, Lucas has repeatedly warned that programs or policies aimed at helping specific groups, such as Black people or women, are unlawful under Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 if they exclude others.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但在采访和公开讲话中，卢卡斯一再警告说，根据 1964 年《民权法案》第七章，旨在帮助黑人或妇女等特定群体的计划或政策如果排除其他群体，则属于非法行为。

contentParagraphs.en: "Regardless of what has happened before, the way to stop discriminating based on race is to stop discriminating based on race. The end. Full stop," Lucas said at the Fortune Workplace Innovation Summit earlier this month. "I think that that's a more beautiful vision of our country, and I think it's consistent with the text of the statute."
contentParagraphs.zh: “无论以前发生过什么，停止基于种族的歧视的方法就是停止基于种族的歧视。结束。完全停止，”卢卡斯本月早些时候在《财富》工作场所创新峰会上表示。 “我认为这是我们国家更美好的愿景，我认为这与法规的文本是一致的。”

contentParagraphs.en: A roadmap for addressing discrimination
contentParagraphs.zh: 解决歧视问题的路线图

contentParagraphs.en: The 1979 regulation the EEOC seeks to rescind was issued with this very dilemma in mind: Can a company remedy discrimination by giving special consideration to those who were deprived of opportunities in the past?
contentParagraphs.zh: 平等就业机会委员会寻求废除的 1979 年法规就是在考虑到这一困境的情况下发布的：公司能否通过特别考虑那些过去被剥夺机会的人来纠正歧视？

contentParagraphs.en: The answer back then was yes. The agency gave the go-ahead for mentoring programs and even hiring targets.
contentParagraphs.zh: 当时的答案是肯定的。该机构批准了指导计划甚至招聘目标。

contentParagraphs.en: "The EEOC says you can take some of these voluntary efforts, even though they will be race- or gender-conscious," says Chai Feldblum, who served on the commission during the Obama and first Trump administrations. "This is the EEOC giving employers the roadmap of how they can take race and gender into account in a positive way and not violate the law."
contentParagraphs.zh: “平等就业机会委员会表示，你可以采取一些自愿的努力，即使它们会考虑到种族或性别，”在奥巴马和特朗普第一届政府期间曾在该委员会任职的柴·费尔德布鲁姆 (Chai Feldblum) 说。 “平等就业机会委员会为雇主提供了如何以积极的方式考虑种族和性别而不违反法律的路线图。”

contentParagraphs.en: The guidelines, issued in January 1979, made clear that companies first had to document a problem, and then come up with a reasonable and time-limited plan for how to increase the number of minorities or women in their ranks.
contentParagraphs.zh: 1979 年 1 月发布的指导方针明确规定，公司首先必须记录问题，然后就如何增加少数族裔或女性员工的数量提出合理且有时限的计划。

contentParagraphs.en: Five months later, the Supreme Court embraced that roadmap. In a 5-2 decision known as Weber, the court found that an affirmative action plan to remedy past discrimination was lawful provided it did not "unnecessarily trammel the interests of white employees" and that it was temporary.
contentParagraphs.zh: 五个月后，最高法院接受了该路线图。在一项被称为“韦伯”的判决中，法院认为纠正过去歧视的平权行动计划是合法的，只要它不会“不必要地束缚白人雇员的利益”，而且它是暂时的。

contentParagraphs.en: In 1987, the court issued another decision, known as Johnson, extending protection to efforts aimed at helping women.
contentParagraphs.zh: 1987 年，法院发布了另一项名为“约翰逊”的判决，将保护范围扩大到旨在帮助妇女的努力。

contentParagraphs.en: Now known as the Weber-Johnson standard, it's still the law regardless of what happens with the EEOC's 1979 regulation, says Feldblum. But for how long, she's not sure.
contentParagraphs.zh: Feldblum 表示，现在被称为韦伯-约翰逊标准，无论 EEOC 1979 年法规发生什么变化，它仍然是法律。但要持续多久，她并不确定。

contentParagraphs.en: "I think the Supreme Court is just waiting for a case that might allow them to overturn those two important cases," she says.
contentParagraphs.zh: “我认为最高法院只是在等待一个可能让他们推翻这两起重要案件的案件，”她说。

contentParagraphs.en: How data has helped root out discrimination
contentParagraphs.zh: 数据如何帮助根除歧视

contentParagraphs.en: The more imminent change, assuming the EEOC's proposals go forward, is the demise of the agency's annual collection of employee demographics. Usually, the data collection begins in late spring. So far this year, there's been no word of it.
contentParagraphs.zh: 假设平等就业机会委员会的提案得以推进，更迫在眉睫的变化是该机构不再收集员工人口统计数据。通常，数据收集在春末开始。今年到目前为止，还没有任何消息。

contentParagraphs.en: Since the 1960s, the EEOC has recovered billions of dollars for workers who have suffered discrimination on the job, and in many cases, EEO-1 data played a key role.
contentParagraphs.zh: 自 20 世纪 60 年代以来，平等就业机会委员会已为在工作中遭受歧视的工人追回了数十亿美元，在许多情况下，EEO-1 数据发挥了关键作用。

contentParagraphs.en: "It's one of the first things that you can look at as you're trying to learn more," says Karla Gilbride, who served as the EEOC's general counsel during the Biden administration.
contentParagraphs.zh: “当你试图了解更多信息时，这是你首先要考虑的事情之一，”在拜登政府期间担任平等就业机会委员会总法律顾问的卡拉吉尔布赖德说。

contentParagraphs.en: Protecting U.S. workers from unlawful discrimination — already a hard task — could become significantly harder if the government no longer has that data within arm's reach, Gilbride says. Having to subpoena data would make enforcement far more laborious and less efficient.
contentParagraphs.zh: 吉尔布赖德说，如果政府不再掌握这些数据，保护美国工人免受非法歧视本来就很艰巨的任务可能会变得更加困难。必须传唤数据将使执法更加费力且效率更低。

contentParagraphs.en: A lawsuit against Bass Pro Shops
contentParagraphs.zh: 针对 Bass Pro Shops 的诉讼

contentParagraphs.en: Consider the lawsuit against Bass Pro Shops, first filed in 2011.
contentParagraphs.zh: 以 2011 年首次提起的针对 Bass Pro Shops 的诉讼为例。

contentParagraphs.en: The EEOC alleged the company, formally known as Bass Pro Outdoor World, discriminated against Black and Hispanic job applicants by not hiring them — not just at one store, but across the country, even in places with sizable Black and Hispanic populations.
contentParagraphs.zh: 平等就业机会委员会指控，该公司（正式名称为 Bass Pro Outdoor World）歧视黑人和西班牙裔求职者，不雇用他们——不仅是在一家商店，而是在全国各地，甚至在黑人和西班牙裔人口众多的地方。

contentParagraphs.en: "Store by store by store, sort of the same idea, where you had areas that had a significant number of Blacks and Latinos, and either zero or very few at the stores," says David Lopez, who was the EEOC's general counsel at the time and now leads the Civil Rights, Migration and Workplace Law Initiative at Arizona State University.
contentParagraphs.zh: 时任平等就业机会委员会总法律顾问、现任亚利桑那州立大学民权、移民和工作场所法律倡议负责人的大卫·洛佩兹 (David Lopez) 表示：“每家商店的想法都差不多，那里有大量黑人和拉丁裔，但商店里的黑人和拉丁裔人数要么为零，要么很少。”

contentParagraphs.en: A Bass Pro Shops Outdoor World retail store in Irvine, Calif.
contentParagraphs.zh: 加利福尼亚州欧文市的一家 Bass Pro Shops Outdoor World 零售店。

contentParagraphs.en: Jeff Gritchen/MediaNews Group/Orange County Register via Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: 杰夫·格里琴/媒体新闻组/奥兰治县注册通过盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: Jeff Gritchen/MediaNews Group/Orange County Register via Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: 杰夫·格里琴/媒体新闻组/奥兰治县注册通过盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: The EEOC saw that pattern because it had Bass Pro's demographic data on file. Government investigators could easily compare the outdoor gear shop to other retailers in the same counties. They could also compare Bass Pro's workforce to the available pool of workers in the surrounding areas.
contentParagraphs.zh: 平等就业机会委员会看到了这种模式，因为它有 Bass Pro 的人口统计数据存档。政府调查人员可以轻松地将户外装备店与同一县的其他零售商进行比较。他们还可以将 Bass Pro 的劳动力与周边地区的可用工人池进行比较。

contentParagraphs.en: While the data by itself could not prove discrimination, Lopez says it was a green light to agency investigators to dig further.
contentParagraphs.zh: 虽然数据本身并不能证明存在歧视，但洛佩兹表示，这为机构调查人员进一步挖掘开了绿灯。

contentParagraphs.en: "Because they had a reason to investigate, they were able to discover that there were managerial comments that were reflective of d
contentParagraphs.zh: “因为他们有理由进行调查，所以他们能够发现管理层的评论反映了

articles.title: Residents flee Beirut’s southern suburbs as Israel orders strikes
articles.summary: Videos show gridlocked traffic in the southern suburbs of Beirut as residents rushed to evacuate the Dahiyeh area.
articles.titleZh: 以色列下令罢工，居民逃离贝鲁特南郊
articles.summaryZh: 视频显示，贝鲁特南郊交通拥堵，居民纷纷撤离达希耶地区。

contentParagraphs.en: Residents flee Beirut’s southern suburbs as Israel orders strikesNewsFeedVideos show gridlocked traffic in the southern suburbs of Beirut as residents rushed to evacuate the Dahiyeh area following Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s order that the military strike neighbourhoods associated with Hezbollah.Published On 1 Jun 2026
contentParagraphs.zh: 以色列下令罢工，居民逃离贝鲁特南郊新闻动态视频显示，以色列总理本杰明·内塔尼亚胡下令军事打击与真主党有关的社区后，居民纷纷撤离达希耶地区，贝鲁特南郊交通陷入拥堵。 发布于 2026 年 6 月 1 日
